summary: All critical and major issues from round 1 are resolved. _last_flow_time now correctly tracks all traffic before the is_target_domain guard; naming is consistent; restart failure activates cooldown; test now uses module constants and mocks memory correctly. One minor observation on test completeness remains.

suggestions.suggestion: test_cooldown_blocks_restart now mocks _get_process_memory_mb and uses module constants — a clear improvement. However mock_restart.assert_not_called() is trivially true: nothing in the test body calls any code that would invoke _restart_mitmproxy, so the assertion cannot fail regardless of implementation. The test effectively verifies that the cooldown condition arithmetic evaluates correctly (which is fine), but it does not exercise the actual _monitor_loop branching. Acceptable for this scope.
suggestions.benefit: Optional improvement: extract the guard-check block from _monitor_loop into a helper and test that helper directly, or drive the monitor via a test-controlled stop_event. Would make test_cooldown_blocks_restart a true behavioral test. Not required for approval.
